### Correctness

- Polars: pass (32/32)
- Booster: pass (32/32)


## Single-Key `var` Evidence

`var` scale on the Rust-first path because each worker accumulates mergeable `(count, mean, m2)` state and Rayon only merges those partial states at the end.
When the dataset is smaller or the group count is low, the fixed overhead from NumPy contiguity/copy work, the PythonâRust boundary, and Series materialization eats into that gain even though the kernel itself remains parallel.

| Workload | Agg | Sort | Backend | Execution | Cold | Warm |
|----------|-----|------|---------|-----------|------|------|
| standard | `var` | True | pandas | `pandas.groupby.var` | 27.28 Âą 2.28 (24.81..31.33) | 22.87 Âą 1.92 (21.18..26.87) |
| standard | `var` | True | polars | `polars.group_by.agg(var)` | 20.45 Âą 2.30 (17.64..28.21) | 17.64 Âą 1.40 (15.92..20.75) |

| standard | `var` | False | pandas | `pandas.groupby.var` | 26.36 Âą 3.30 (23.49..36.24) | 25.22 Âą 2.75 (21.91..30.38) |

### Booster conversion vs compute breakdown

The table below isolates the Rust-first Booster path for the same single-key datasets. `local_build`, `merge`, `reorder`, and `materialize` come from the internal Rust profiling hook, while the Python-side phases measure post-kernel normalization and final pandas Series assembly.

| Workload | Agg | Sort | Execution | Prepare inputs | Local build | Merge | Reorder | Materialize | Python normalize | Series build | Rust total | Total pipeline | Partial groups | Final groups | Partial/final |
